questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f: R \rightarrow R$ be defined as $f(x)=(2 x-3 \pi)^3+\frac{4 x}{3}+\cos x$ and $g=f^{-1}$, then find the value of $7 g^{\prime}(2 \pi)+3 g^{\prime \prime}(2 \pi)$.
questions.solution:
$$
g^{\prime}(f(x))=\frac{1}{f^{\prime}(x)} \Rightarrow g^{\prime}(2 \pi)=\frac{1}{f^{\prime}\left(\frac{3 \pi}{2}\right)}=\frac{1}{\left(\frac{7}{3}\right)}=\frac{3}{7}
$$

And
$$
\begin{aligned}
& g^{\prime \prime}(f(x))=\frac{-f^{\prime \prime}(x)}{\left(f^{\prime}(x)\right)^3} \\
& g^{\prime \prime}(2 \pi)=\frac{-f^{\prime \prime}\left(\frac{3 \pi}{2}\right)}{\left(f^{\prime}\left(\frac{3 \pi}{2}\right)\right)^3}=0
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\therefore \quad 7 g^{\prime}(2 \pi)+3 g^{\prime \prime}(2 \pi)=3
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f: R \rightarrow R$ be a function defined by $f(x)=x^3+2 x^2+3 x+2$ and $g$ be the inverse function of $f$. If $\left.\frac{d}{d x}(g(g(g(x))))\right|_{x=24}=\frac{p}{q}$, where $p, q$ are co-prime, then find the value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { 9) } f(-1)=0, f(0)=2 \text { and } f(2)=24, f^{\prime}(x)=3 x^2+4 x+3 \\ & \frac{d}{d x}(g(g(g(x))))=g^{\prime}(g(g(x))) \cdot g^{\prime}(g(x)) \cdot g^{\prime}(x) \\ & \left.\frac{d}{d x}(g(g(g(x))))\right|_{x=24}=g^{\prime}(g(g(24))) \cdot g^{\prime}(g(24)) \cdot g^{\prime}(24)=g^{\prime}(g(2)) \cdot g^{\prime}(2) \cdot \frac{1}{f^{\prime}(2)} \\ & =g^{\prime}(0) \cdot \frac{1}{f^{\prime}(0)} \cdot \frac{1}{f^{\prime}(2)}=\frac{1}{f^{\prime}(-1)} \cdot \frac{1}{f^{\prime}(0)} \cdot \frac{1}{f^{\prime}(2)}=\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{23}=\frac{1}{138} \equiv \frac{p}{q} \\ & \therefore \quad p \quad p=139\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $x=4 t^3+3, \quad y=4+3 t^4$ and $\frac{\left(\frac{d^2 x}{d y^2}\right)}{\left(\frac{d x}{d y}\right)^n}$ is a constant, then find the value of $\frac{4}{5}+\frac{4}{5 n}+\frac{4}{5 n^2}+\ldots .$. upto infinity.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \frac{d x}{d y} & =\frac{\frac{d x}{d t}}{\frac{d y}{d t}}=\frac{12 t^2}{12 t^3}=\frac{1}{t} \\ \frac{d^2 x}{d y^2} & =\frac{d}{d t}\left(\frac{1}{t}\right) \times\left(\frac{d t}{d y}\right)=\frac{-1}{t^2} \times \frac{1}{12 t^3}=\frac{-1}{12 t^5} \\ \frac{\left(\frac{d^2 x}{d y^2}\right)}{\left(\frac{d x}{d y}\right)^n} & =\text { constant } \\ \therefore \quad & n=5 \\ \therefore \quad \text { Sum } & =\frac{\frac{4}{5}}{1-\frac{1}{n}}=\frac{\frac{4}{5}}{1-\frac{1}{5}}=1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If $x \int_0^x \sin (f(t)) d t=(x+2) \int_0^x t \sin (f(t)) d t$ where $x>0$, then find the value of $f^{\prime}(x) \cot (f(x))+\frac{3}{1+x}$
questions.solution:
Differentiate both sides
$$
\begin{aligned}
& x \sin (f(x))+\int_0^x \sin (f(t)) d t=(x+2) \sin (f(x))+\int_0^x t \sin (f(t)) d t \\
& \therefore \quad x^2 \sin (f(x))+x \sin (f(x))=\int_0^x \sin (f(t)) d t-\int_0^x t \sin (f(t)) d t \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$

Again differentiate
$$
\begin{aligned}
& x^2 \cos (f(x)) f^{\prime}(x)+2 x \sin (f(x))+\sin (f(x))+x \cos (f(x)) f^{\prime}(x)=\sin (f(x))-x \sin (f(x)) \\
& =x(x+1) \cos (f(x))+3 x \sin (f(x))=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad f^{\prime}(x) \cot (f(x))+\frac{3}{1+x}=0
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
